hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""' | { read -r f; echo "$f" | grep -qE '\.py$' && cd /Users/sabareeswarans/Projects_26/PixelStream && /Users/sabareeswarans/pinokio/new/bin/miniconda/bin/uv run ruff check --fix "$f" && /Users/sabareeswarans/pinokio/new/bin/miniconda/bin/uv run ruff format "$f"; } 2>/dev/null || true

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""' | { read -r f; echo "$f" | grep -qE '\.py$' && cd /Users/sabareeswarans/Projects_26/PixelStream && /Users/sabareeswarans/pinokio/new/bin/miniconda/bin/uv run mypy "$f" --ignore-missing-imports 2>&1 | tail -5; } || true

hooks.prompt: You are the qa-engineer agent for PixelStream (see .claude/agents/qa-engineer.md). A Python file was just edited. The hook input is available in your context. Extract the file path from the tool_input, read the file, and run your QA checklist: correctness, pipeline-specific checks, missing tests. Output a compact QA CHECK report. If no issues, say '✓ No issues found'. Be terse — max 10 lines.

You are the data-engineer agent for PixelStream (see .claude/agents/data-engineer.md). A file was just edited. The hook input has the file path. ONLY act if the file path contains one of: batch_handler, delta_writer, schemas, frame_producer. Read the file and run your data contract checklist: schema compatibility, Kafka message integrity, Delta write safety, data invariants. Output a compact DATA ENGINEER CHECK report. If the file is not a data-layer file, output nothing and exit immediately.
